summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing covering multiple cases including property maintenance continuances, a Dollar General sidewalk variance, a construction setback variance for a duplex in Greenstone Trace, a dumpster placement appeal for Sprinkles ice cream shop, and an appeal/variance request for a new single family home on agricultural property on Woodville Pike. The board handled procedural matters including appointing a temporary secretary and approving prior meeting minutes.
decisions: Granted continuance of BZA 2021-03 (property maintenance) until June
Granted continuance of BZA 2021-007 (property maintenance) until June
Granted variance for BZA 2022-001 (Dollar General sidewalk requirement under 12.08A)
Approved Resolution RO21-2022 denying variance to construct 6-foot privacy fence in front yard at 1910 State Route 28
Granted variance for BZA 2022-003 (Greenstone Trace setback variance of 3.8 feet due to foundation contractor error)
Granted continuance for BZA 2022-004 (Sprinkles dumpster appeal) to allow resubmission as variance request
Board entered executive session regarding BZA 2022-005 (Woodville Pike home appeal)
action_items: Sprinkles owner to resubmit application as a variance request instead of an appeal for dumpster placement (BZA 2022-004)
Harry Hobart to coordinate notification timeline for Sprinkles special hearing with newspaper publication deadlines
Derek to serve as pro tem secretary for meeting notes
Appointment of permanent BZA secretary to be continued to next meeting
Board of Trustees to address sidewalk/ODOT responsibility issue at future trustee meetings
key_amounts: 3.8 feet variance requested for Greenstone Trace duplex setback (46.6 feet actual vs 50 foot required)
Half million dollar home proposed on Woodville Pike agricultural property
15 acres - size of Humphrey property on Woodville Pike
5 acres - minimum lot size for agricultural exemption under Section 3.02
50 foot front yard setback required for Greenstone Trace lots
6 foot tall privacy fence denied at 1910 State Route 28
notable_topics: Property maintenance enforcement along Goshen Road with court involvement
Dollar General sidewalk variance due to ODOT refusing to let applicant maintain sidewalks in state right-of-way
Foundation contractor error causing setback violation at Greenstone Trace development
Sprinkles ice cream shop dumpster placement dispute - front yard prohibition and appeal vs variance procedural confusion
Agricultural exemption interpretation under Section 3.02 for new home construction on Woodville Pike
Debate over whether agricultural-exempt structures can be used to deny placement of new principal structure
Small business owner frustration with zoning application complexity
attendees: Chris (resident at 6964 Greenstone Trace Unit B)
Scott Chastain (1905 Main Street, property owner in support of Sprinkles)
Joe Braun (attorney for Humphrey/Woodville Pike applicant)
Mr. Humphrey Sr. (former township trustee and county commissioner, Scott's father)
Casey McLean (neighbor across street on Woodville Pike)
Tom Curry (referenced as potential secretary, absent)
notes: The Sprinkles dumpster case became heated with the small business owner expressing significant frustration about the complexity of zoning procedures and feeling she was given incorrect guidance on which application to file. The Woodville Pike case involved a substantive legal dispute between attorney Joe Braun and Zoning Inspector Hobart over interpretation of Section 3.02 agricultural exemption - whether existing agricultural-exempt structures can be considered when reviewing placement of a new principal structure. The board entered executive session on the Woodville Pike appeal, and the transcript appears to be part 1 of 2 suggesting the meeting continued. The Pledge of Allegiance was repeated multiple times in the transcript, likely a recording artifact.
title: BZA 3.21.22 1 of 2.mp4
